own_review_points: SpannerFormatFunction.render: switch(temporalType) on a null temporalType (getSqlTemporalType may return null) throws NPE — no null guard/default
var_pop/stddev_pop registered as E[X^2]-E[X]^2 emulation patterns: numerically unstable (catastrophic cancellation) and questionable if Spanner has native VAR_POP/STDDEV_POP
locate ternary pattern evaluates strpos(substr(?2,?3),?1) twice
right pattern substr(?1,-?2): negative-offset edge semantics when ?2 exceeds string length
Human: SpannerDialect could emulate local timestamps via custom JdbcType implementations (UTC shift on JVM side) instead of skipping the timezone tests
notes: Three substantive grounded points: the SpannerFormatFunction null-temporalType NPE gap (v8 mis-states it as 'IllegalArgumentException with null message' — a switch on a null enum NPEs — but the underlying defect is real), the var_pop/stddev_pop numerical-instability + emulation-vs-native concern (well reasoned, the PR explicitly registers the emulation), and the double-strpos in locate. The rest (SpannerTruncFunction default-case — v8 itself concedes null IS guarded to numeric, log comment, castPattern restating, EPOCH restating/praise, test-split @SkipForDialect suggestion) are trivial. Missed the human's custom-JdbcType design suggestion.
